summary: Joel describes a practical problem at Fog Creek: their office library has grown so disorganized that it's easier to buy a new copy from Amazon than find a book they already own. He's looking for software that can scan ISBN barcodes, look up catalog information online, and print spine stickers with Library of Congress numbers so books can be properly shelved. The post is essentially a public request for software recommendations, directed at his readership.
key_points: Fog Creek's office library has grown large enough that finding books is harder than reordering them from Amazon
Joel wants a barcode-scanning workflow that automates cataloging: scan ISBN, fetch metadata, print spine labels
The post illustrates how even simple organizational problems at small companies can become friction points that waste money
money_quotes: It's actually easier to order a new copy of a book from Amazon than to find a book we already have!
key_insight: When finding something you already own costs more effort than buying it again, you have an organizational problem worth solving with software.
